title: Sunday Session Brief - July 19, 2026. Lowest Source Consensus of the Series. GBP/USD Now Carries Four Independent Signals Against It. CAD COT Pushes to a Fresh Extreme.
thesis: This was the lowest-consensus week of the series so far, with sources disagreeing on the basic regime more than in any prior brief and one headline claim treated with real skepticism after it was traced back to a report already covered four days earlier rather than a fresh development. Given that thin and disputed macro picture, positioning and technicals carried more analytical weight than usual this session. EUR/USD was the one pair with genuine unanimous agreement across every source, bearish, while most other pairs showed a wide, low-conviction spread. USD/CAD long holds at Medium with its Thesis Invalidation question from the prior Wednesday's central bank decision still formally unresolved; the technical and positioning case remains intact regardless, with CAD's net-short COT position pushing to a fresh series extreme and D1 and H4 momentum both reading Strong to Very Strong. GBP/USD short holds at Low-Medium, but the case for caution is broader than previously stated: COT short-covering is now a confirmed two-report trend, stacking on a structural fundamental headwind flagged the prior session and a political tailwind question that remains unresolved. This session adds a fourth, independent signal that deserves equal weight rather than a footnote: the pair sits in a genuine technical consolidation zone, with three of four ADX timeframes reading Weak and only the H4 timeframe marginally confirming trend strength. None of the four signals individually triggers the formal Thesis Invalidation, but the accumulation across positioning, fundamentals, and technicals in the same direction is the more important read than any single data point. NZD/USD short holds at Low-Medium for a third consecutive session, with H4 momentum remaining Very Strong against the position and no fresh currency futures positioning data available this week to check the other side of the argument; its own Thesis Invalidation, a decisive broad risk-on move with safe-haven currency weakness, has still not triggered. USD/JPY remains the single cleanest unanimous call across sources this week on direction and key level, though it stays commentary only per the standing rule against positioning in that pair. Gold and silver showed a genuine split in source view this week between geopolitical-premium bullishness and real-yield-driven bearishness, with the established framework from prior sessions carried forward unchanged: a durable long case requires a paused or cutting central bank alongside still-rising inflation, a combination not currently in play. The ECB's Thursday rate decision and press conference stand out as the week's single most consequential scheduled event and the most likely catalyst to reset the tone for the data-dense back half of the week, which also includes Canada CPI, UK CPI, Australian employment, Japanese CPI, US Q2 GDP, and US Core PCE.
highlights: Lowest cross-source consensus of the series; one stale headline traced and discarded
EUR/USD the sole unanimous call this week; most other pairs low-conviction and split
USD/CAD holds Medium, CAD COT net-short pushes to a fresh series extreme
GBP/USD short now carries four independent caution signals, none individually decisive
NZD/USD short enters a third session of adverse momentum with no fresh positioning data
ECB Thursday flagged as the week's single most consequential scheduled event

title: Friday Brief - July 17, 2026. Fed Settles Into a Long Pause. GBP/USD and NZD/USD Shorts Both Downgraded on a Real Structural Headwind. BoC Leaves the USD/CAD Thesis Genuinely Unresolved.
thesis: This week resolved into a genuine Fed long pause rather than a clean directional outcome. Both June CPI and PPI came in cooler than expected, largely on falling energy prices but with real core softening too, effectively removing the chance of a near-term hike and pulling September odds below 60 percent from more than fully priced earlier in the week on the Iran-driven oil spike. The dollar fell to a one-month low. Warsh's Senate testimony on Wednesday was confirmed word for word identical to his House remarks two days earlier, offering no incremental signal in either direction, reaffirming only that the Committee has no tolerance for persistently elevated inflation without committing to a path. Thursday's Retail Sales complicated the clean weak-consumer narrative that would have justified the desk's own pre-set trigger for fresh dollar shorts: headline growth was almost entirely a gasoline-price effect, but core retail sales rose a solid 0.5 percent, aided by Amazon Prime Day and World Cup-related spending, meaning the trigger did not fire. The Bank of Canada held its policy rate at 2.25 percent on Wednesday as expected; the full statement text, reviewed directly rather than through any single source's characterization, is genuinely neutral, describing the current rate as appropriate and closing with fully standard two-sided language about being prepared to adjust policy as needed, with growth actually described more positively than expected and CAD's depreciation attributed by the Bank itself to a widening yield differential rather than a policy signal. This did not deliver the explicit hawkish removal of easing guidance the USD/CAD Thesis Invalidation specifically required, but it was not a dovish signal either, leaving the setup's core question genuinely unresolved rather than confirmed or invalidated. The dominant and most consequential development this week is a reframing of the entire FX picture: with the dollar itself unlikely to have a large standalone move under a genuine pause, currencies already in an active tightening cycle, specifically RBNZ, RBA, and BoE, are expected to keep outperforming it directly, a conclusion reached independently of any AI source. This is a direct, structural challenge to two of the desk's three active positions. GBP/USD short is downgraded from Medium to Low-Medium: sterling has a genuine, specific catalyst this week in reports that incoming PM Burnham is considering a fiscally conservative Chancellor, which rallied both the currency and gilts on reduced budget-crisis risk, stacking a real political tailwind for GBP on top of the structural hawkish-outperformer argument working against the short, while the technical picture remains genuinely unresolved across timeframes. NZD/USD short is downgraded from Medium to Low-Medium on the same structural logic applied even more directly, since RBNZ's hike is the cleanest hawkish central bank story of the past two weeks, compounded by H4 momentum intensifying to its strongest reading yet against the position for a second consecutive session, even though the position's formal Thesis Invalidation, a decisive broad risk-on move with safe-haven currency weakness, has still not technically triggered. Gold's proprietary sell bias enters a fourth consecutive session unchanged, with the underlying mechanism explained in full this week: cooling inflation removes gold's rationale as a hedge rather than requiring a rally, and the only genuine long-term buy trigger the desk would recognize is the Fed staying on pause or cutting while inflation is still rising, the opposite of the cooling-inflation, patient-Fed combination actually in play. USD/JPY remains the one clean, undisputed theme of the week, the single currency that has not benefited from the broad dollar softness, still commentary only per the standing rule. Weekend Iran-US headline risk remains exactly as live as it has been for three consecutive Fridays, with no scheduled central bank event to backstop it before Monday's open.
highlights: Fed settles into a long pause - core retail sales denies the desk's own USD-short trigger
BoC holds at 2.25%, statement genuinely neutral, USD/CAD Thesis Invalidation left unresolved
Warsh Senate testimony identical to House remarks, zero new guidance
GBP/USD short downgraded to Low-Medium on a real political catalyst plus structural headwind
NZD/USD short downgraded to Low-Medium, H4 momentum hits strongest reading yet against it
Gold sell bias enters fourth consecutive session, full mechanism explained this week

title: Midweek Brief - July 15, 2026. Whipsaw Week: CPI Reverses Escalation-Driven Dollar Strength. USD/CAD's Strongest Technical Setup of the Series Faces a Live BoC Test Today.
thesis: This is a pre-event snapshot, published ahead of today's BoC decision and Warsh Senate testimony, and should be read as a state of the world that is expected to move within hours rather than a settled read. Iran-US conflict re-escalated hard over the weekend: US forces struck Iranian military targets including missile systems, drones, and related infrastructure, Iran retaliated against US regional interests, Hormuz traffic fell to near-minimal levels, and Trump reimposed a blockade on Iranian oil shipping plus a 20 percent toll on vessels transiting under US naval escort, pushing oil back toward 80 dollars. Monday's dollar strength on that escalation, which briefly pushed September Fed hike pricing to fully priced and July meeting odds to 43 percent from 22 percent on Sunday, fully reversed by Tuesday when June CPI showed core inflation cooling for the first time this cycle, the second consecutive month of genuinely cooler data after the earlier NFP miss. The desk's own proprietary framework stays deliberately disciplined against chasing that dollar weakness with fresh shorts, citing two live risks that could reverse it just as fast: a hawkish surprise from Warsh's Senate testimony today, following his House testimony yesterday, or the reheating Iran conflict keeping an oil-driven inflation floor under the picture regardless of what core CPI just showed. The plan on record is to wait for Thursday's Retail Sales as confirmation that the CPI cooling reflects genuine consumer weakness rather than an energy base effect before adding fresh USD-short exposure. One source was discarded entirely for the second time in three sessions on the same class of error, again naming the wrong sitting Fed Chair alongside a DXY level roughly three points off every surviving read, now treated as a pattern warranting elevated scrutiny going forward rather than a fresh isolated catch each time. USD/CAD long carries the strongest technical setup of the entire series into today's live event risk: momentum has gone Very Strong across H1, H4, and D1 simultaneously, a genuinely rare triple-timeframe confirmation, landing on the exact day the BoC decides whether its hold explicitly removes easing guidance, the precise condition of this setup's own Thesis Invalidation. GBP/USD short holds at Medium on a genuinely mixed momentum picture, H1 improved to Strong while H4 slipped to Neutral and D1 remains Weak, with no fundamental change and UK CPI tomorrow the nearest domestic test. NZD/USD short's Thesis Invalidation did not trigger today, since the tape does not show a clean, decisive risk-on move with broad safe-haven currency weakness, but the position remains genuinely uncomfortable to hold on technicals, with Strong momentum across H1, H4, and D1 running against it for a second consecutive session. Gold's proprietary sell bias, now in its third consecutive session, is explicitly conditioned on today's Warsh testimony rather than stated as an unqualified view, with a genuine tail scenario flagged where a Fed pause or cut alongside rising yields could send gold parabolic rather than lower. Forward calendar through the rest of the week: Thursday brings US Retail Sales, the desk's own trigger point for potential fresh USD-short entries, alongside UK CPI and US Initial Jobless Claims; Friday brings US Housing Starts, Industrial Production, and Michigan Consumer Sentiment as the closing data points of an exceptionally dense week.
highlights: Pre-event snapshot - BoC decision and Warsh Senate testimony still pending as published
Weekend Iran-US escalation: US strikes, Iran retaliation, Hormuz near-minimal, oil toward $80
Core CPI cools for first time this cycle, fully reversing Monday's escalation-driven dollar strength
USD/CAD momentum Very Strong across H1/H4/D1 simultaneously, rarest reading in the series
One source discarded twice in three sessions on the same wrong-Fed-Chair error - now a flagged pattern
Desk stays disciplined: no fresh USD shorts until Thursday Retail Sales confirms genuine softness

title: Sunday Session Brief - July 12, 2026. Quiet Week Into a Loaded Calendar. NZD/USD Formalized as a Contrarian Short. One Source Discarded on a Checkable Factual Error.
thesis: This was a genuinely quiet week functioning as a coiled spring ahead of the heaviest calendar stretch since the mid-June FOMC and BoJ resolution week: US CPI, a BoC decision that directly tests the USD/CAD Thesis Invalidation, an ECB decision, Warsh's Congressional testimony, and both UK CPI and employment all land within a five day window. One source's entire output was discarded this session on a clean, checkable factual error rather than a judgment call - it referred to the sitting Fed Chair by a name that has not held the role since May and named the wrong RBA and SNB governors as well, on top of a DXY level roughly six points off every other read. COT data is fresh again this week, resolving the staleness flagged two weeks ago, with the report covering July 7. USD/CAD long recovers modestly to Medium after Friday's Low-Medium flag: oil's pullback to roughly 70 dollars partially reverses the direct CAD tailwind from Friday's escalation spike, and CAD's fresh COT extreme of -173,126 held through that spike without meaningful short covering, though Wednesday's BoC decision is a same-week binary event directly on the setup's own Thesis Invalidation. GBP/USD short holds at Medium with levels refreshed to updated stop and target zones; two soft signals are worth watching together rather than individually, real COT short-covering this week and a modest easing in Fed hike odds, neither disqualifying the thesis on its own. NZD/USD, flagged as the board's cleanest bullish setup across the two prior sessions, is formalized this session as a short rather than a long - a deliberate, explicitly contrarian fade against the week's unanimous bullish source consensus and Strong H4/D1 momentum, built on the view that a hawkish RBNZ hike already roughly 80 percent priced functions as a sell-the-news event once delivered, and that broader safe-haven positioning into JPY and CHF is the dominant force in the pair rather than the rate decision itself. USD/JPY remains commentary only; overnight yen strength on GPIF domestic-reallocation headlines is read as likely tactical rather than the start of a structural move, and the standing rule against positioning in this pair is unchanged regardless of signal quality.
highlights: One source discarded on a checkable factual error - wrong Fed Chair, wrong RBA/SNB governors
COT data fresh again (July 7 report), CAD short at a fresh series extreme
USD/CAD recovers to Medium, BoC decision Wed is a direct live TI test
GBP/USD holds Medium, levels refreshed to updated stop/target zones
NZD/USD formalized as a deliberate contrarian short, against source and momentum consensus
Heaviest calendar week since mid-June: CPI, BoC, ECB, Warsh testimony, UK data

title: Friday Brief - July 10, 2026. Iran Strikes Oil Tankers, US Hits Back. Sources Miss the Story. Both Active Setups Downgraded on Real Deterioration.
thesis: The dominant story today was geopolitical, not monetary, and most source models missed its weight. Iran struck oil tankers in the Strait of Hormuz and the US answered with airstrikes on Iranian targets, sending WTI back above $73 and erasing roughly two weeks of decline in a single move, with the timing tied to a former Iranian supreme leader's funeral that drew large crowds and left the door open to further hostilities. Two of four source models still framed the session as generic risk-on; two others carried DXY levels 2 to 4 points off every other read and were discarded, along with one unconfirmed narrative about a Japanese pension-fund driven yen short squeeze. RBNZ's hawkish hike, confirmed midweek, was formally finalized at OCR 2.50 percent with explicit language leaving the door open to further tightening, and is being read by some as a signal other hawkish-leaning central banks, ECB included, might look past the recent oil pullback and tighten again regardless of energy prices easing. Both active setups were genuinely downgraded rather than defended by default. USD/CAD long moved from Medium to Low-Medium: D1 momentum remains Very Strong and technically intact, but today's oil spike is a direct, live tailwind for CAD working against the pair through the same channel the original thesis leans on, stacked on Wednesday's bearish daily-bias flag, with Canada employment landing as a same-day binary test. GBP/USD short moved from Medium-High back to Medium: Wednesday's momentum-based upgrade did not hold, with H1 dropping from Strong to Weak and D1 from Neutral to Weak, even though the fundamental case is unchanged and the Thesis Invalidation did not trigger against Wednesday's FOMC Minutes, which were not dovish. NZD/USD stands out as the cleanest technical and fundamental alignment of the week, confirmed hawkish hike paired with the single strongest ADX reading across the entire pair set, but remains unpositioned pending an explicit Thesis Invalidation per standing discipline. USD/JPY stays commentary only, showing its first real H1 momentum confirmation of the week but with direction still genuinely contested across sources. Gold's proprietary desk view continued favoring sales into rallies for a second consecutive session, against a source consensus leaning neutral to bullish on geopolitical premium. Weekend risk is flagged as elevated given the live, ongoing conflict and no scheduled central bank event to absorb a headline before Monday's open.
highlights: Iran strikes oil tankers, US airstrikes on Iran, WTI back above $73
Majority of sources missed the escalation, framed session as risk-on
USD/CAD downgraded to Low-Medium on oil-driven CAD tailwind
GBP/USD downgraded back to Medium as momentum reversed
NZD/USD cleanest setup on the board, no TI yet, not positioned
Weekend geopolitical risk flagged - no CB event to backstop a headline

title: Midweek Brief - July 8, 2026. RBNZ Delivers Confirmed Hawkish Hike. Majority of Sources Get the Call Wrong. GBP/USD Upgraded on Momentum, FOMC Minutes the Live Test.
thesis: RBNZ delivered a confirmed 25bp hike today, vindicating the hawkish RBNZ arc that has run through this series since May and built on a genuine 3-3 vote split at the prior meeting plus roughly 80% market-implied hike odds. A majority of this session's source models got the actual outcome wrong in opposite directions - one reported a surprise cut to 2.50%, one reported a dovish hold projecting cuts to 3.0% by 2027 - and both were discarded rather than laundered into the brief, alongside one source's entire macro section which cited an internally stale DXY level and a meeting date that had already passed. GBP/USD is upgraded from Medium to Medium-High conviction: the one open caution from the 07-05 brief, Neutral H1 momentum against a Strong H4, has resolved to confirmed Strong on both timeframes, while the Thesis Invalidation faces its first live test this afternoon when FOMC Minutes are released. USD/CAD long carries at Medium conviction with a new caution flag rather than a downgrade - D1 ADX remains Very Strong and essentially unchanged week over week, but today's daily bias table leans against the pair for the first time since the setup was built, on CAD strength from steady Trade Balance data and a broadly softer near-term dollar read across sources. AUD/USD remains unpositioned but is flagged for active monitoring: D1 and W1 momentum have both rebuilt to Strong since the short was closed on 07-05, a genuine technical development, though per standing discipline it is not promoted without an explicit new Thesis Invalidation. USD/JPY stays as commentary only - spot is pressing back toward the 162 handle across every surviving source, but ADX readings are neutral-to-weak across all four timeframes, the same exhaustion signature flagged after the prior intervention bounce, meaning price is making highs without momentum confirmation. Gold is presented with an explicit tension this session: source consensus reads neutral to cautiously bullish, while the desk's own analytical framework continues favoring sales into rallies on the view that the current pullback likely needs an extended consolidation period before a genuine bottom, consistent with prior cycles in this pattern. Week ahead: FOMC Minutes today 18:00 GMT is the immediate catalyst, followed by China CPI/PPI Thursday and a Friday double print of US Non-Farm Payrolls and Canada Employment that directly stress-tests the USD/CAD setup.
highlights: RBNZ hikes 25bp, confirmed - majority of sources called it wrong
GBP/USD upgraded to Medium-High on confirmed H1/H4 momentum
FOMC Minutes today 18:00 GMT - live test of GBP/USD TI
USD/CAD D1 ADX holds 50.84 Very Strong, daily bias flips bearish
AUD/USD momentum rebuilding, watching for a fresh TI, not promoted yet
Proprietary gold view stays a seller against a mixed source consensus

title: Sunday Session Brief - July 5, 2026. NFP Miss Drives Risk-On. GBP/USD Promoted to Top Setup on COT Extreme. AUD/USD Short Thesis Closed.
thesis: June NFP missed badly at +57K vs +110K expected, pulling DXY off its highs toward 100.00-100.85 and reopening the case for earlier Fed cuts than the market was pricing into the print. Three of four source models converge independently on a risk-on, softer-dollar read; one source's macro conclusion was internally stale, still carrying an 80%-hike-by-December narrative from earlier in the week that the print itself invalidates, and was discarded entirely rather than laundered into the brief. Bond yields have NOT confirmed a genuine dovish regime change, consistent with the 07-03 assessment - this stays a checkpoint, not a confirmed reversal, and every bullish-USD-weakness read in this brief carries that ceiling. GBP/USD is promoted to a full Top Setup for the first time this session on the steepest single-week COT short acceleration in the series (-105,719 net, -34,134 WoW, June 23 report) plus a genuine Thesis Invalidation: FOMC Minutes or subsequent US labor data confirming the market is pricing cuts faster than currently expected, with Wednesday's Minutes the near-term test. USD/CAD long carries at Medium conviction unchanged from 07-03 - D1 ADX remains Very Strong at 51.69, the highest D1 reading across the entire pair set, and CAD COT shorts are still building (-13,891 WoW), but the Fed-BoC divergence pillar stays under live stress pending BoC's own guidance. AUD/USD's original short thesis is closed outright rather than carried at a discount: the COT long extreme that built the case has fully unwound to outright net short and is still deepening (-8,887 WoW), today's daily bias consensus is 3-of-4 bullish, and no fresh technical or macro catalyst exists to justify a new short - standing aside until one does, rather than re-entering the same idea at new levels. USD/JPY remains without a position; BoJ intervention is confirmed, the bias table is now unanimously bearish near-term, and medium-term structural bullish case (BoJ real-yield gap) requires actual price stabilization in the 161.50-162.00 zone before it becomes a genuine signal rather than color. One source's COT summary this session also mischaracterized EUR as net short; the CFTC print shows EUR non-commercial positioning net long by over 30,000 contracts, and the CFTC data overrides the AI summary per standing house policy. Week ahead: RBA Tuesday, RBNZ Wednesday (binary vol event for NZD), FOMC Minutes Wednesday, US Core CPI Thursday (the primary risk to the entire risk-on thesis this week), Canada employment Friday (direct stress test for the USD/CAD setup).
highlights: GBP/USD promoted to Top Setup, TI defined for first time
COT still June 23 vintage - June 30 report not yet released
USD/CAD D1 ADX 51.69 - highest reading across the pair set
AUD/USD short thesis closed, not carried - no fresh signal
One source's COT read on EUR contradicted by CFTC data, discarded

title: Friday Brief - July 3, 2026. NFP Badly Misses. Japan Intervenes. Both Active Setups Stress-Tested and Downgraded.
thesis: First genuine test of the hawkish-USD narrative since the June 17 FOMC. June NFP missed badly at +57K vs +110K expected with -74K in prior revisions - worst print in four months. Unemployment fell to 4.2% from 4.3%, a mixed signal more consistent with labor force exit than genuine strength. Japan confirmed spot market intervention after USD/JPY breached 162, a 40-year low - bond yields did not follow the move, the clear tell this was intervention rather than structural dollar weakness. USD/JPY H1 ADX hit 52.19, the most extreme single-timeframe reading recorded in this entire brief series. Warsh, Lagarde, Bailey, and Macklem shared a panel at the ECB Sintra Forum and all four struck a notably softer tone - Warsh explicitly said inflation risks have decreased due to falling oil prices, while offering no forward guidance per his standing framework. Eurozone flash CPI dropped to 2.8% from 3.2% (core to 2.4%), following a softer German print earlier in the week, materially reducing ECB July hike urgency and contradicting prior ECB source commentary about a potential nasty surprise reviving hike odds. Critically, bond yields have NOT confirmed a genuine dovish regime change - SOFR futures remain in hike territory and yields are still up roughly 60bp since March, meaning this is being treated as a checkpoint requiring confirmation, not a confirmed trend reversal. Both active setups were honestly stress-tested rather than defended by default: USD/CAD long downgraded from High to Medium conviction as the Fed-BoC divergence thesis was directly challenged, though D1 ADX barely moved (52.33) and no Thesis Invalidation triggered; AUD/USD short downgraded to Low-Medium as H1/H4 momentum genuinely stalled (dropped to NEUTRAL from STRONG) with sources explicitly split on risk-on vs risk-off interpretation, though D1/W1 medium-term structure remains intact. USD/JPY remains without an active position - analyst's unsolicited lean is toward the intervention fading rather than starting a genuine trend, given no accompanying BoJ policy change, with PM Takaichi's dovish-leaning board appointments flagged as a structural medium-term headwind to the JPY-strength case regardless. Next week's ISM Services Monday and BoC Business Outlook Survey are flagged as the genuine confirmation tests for both the broader dollar narrative and the USD/CAD setup specifically.
highlights: Japan confirms intervention, yields didn't follow
USD/JPY H1 ADX 52.19 - most extreme in series
Sintra: Warsh, Lagarde, Bailey, Macklem all softer
Both active setups honestly downgraded, not defended

title: Midweek Brief - July 1, 2026. Yen at 40-Year Low. Gold Below $4,000. Warsh Speaks at Sintra. NFP Thursday.
thesis: Two historic levels broken simultaneously on Q3 open: USD/JPY at 162.69 is a 40-year low for the yen (above the 2024 high of 161.96, entering 1986 territory) with MoF intervention warnings active and Katayama-Bessent call confirmed but content undisclosed. Gold broke below $4,000 for the first time since the early conflict period on USD strength and reduced geopolitical premium. Dollar entered Q3 at its strongest level since May 2025, posting the best two-week performance since peak Iran-Israel conflict. DXY at 101.1, September hike priced at 72%. German June CPI soft at 2.3% vs 2.6% expected, raising risk of Eurozone CPI downside surprise today and questioning the ECB July hike probability that ECB sources this week said could be revived by a nasty inflation surprise. Oil WTI at $70 essentially pre-conflict levels - the energy-driven inflation justification for the hawkish repricing is mechanically fading and will show in CPI data in 2-3 months. Warsh, Lagarde, and Bailey speaking at ECB Sintra Forum today - Warsh's first public appearance since the FOMC and the real swing factor for near-term USD direction. Analyst assessment explicit: dollar is stretched at current levels, expects pullback, standing aside on new positions pending NFP Thursday (compressed to Thursday due to July 4 US holiday closure). USD/CAD D1 VERY STRONG 52.2, series-high maintained. AUD/USD 4-TF momentum fully restored (H1/H4/D1/W1 all STRONG or VERY STRONG). Canada employment and US NFP both release Thursday 12:30 GMT - double-event compressed window. GBP/USD short signal still awaiting Thesis Invalidation. USD/JPY direction still awaiting explicit input.
highlights: Gold broke below $4,000 for first time
Dollar best two-week run since March conflict peak
German CPI soft 2.3% vs 2.6%, EZ CPI risk today
Warsh at Sintra today, first appearance post-FOMC
NFP Thursday compressed, US closed Friday July 4

title: Sunday Weekly Debrief - June 28, 2026. Two-Week Catch-Up. USD/CAD Series-High Confirmation. USD/JPY Nears 1986 Level. AUD Flips Net Short.
thesis: Two-week brief gap covered following analyst vacation. Dollar rallied over 2 percent on the basket since the June 17 FOMC, six consecutive up days on the Warsh hawkish shock. May PCE confirmed the elevated inflation picture - headline 4.1 percent YoY, core 3.4 percent YoY, both in line with expectations but more than double the Fed target, with personal spending and income both beating. Markets now fully price a September hike with another nearly priced by March 2027. USD/JPY reached 161.92, one tick from the 2024 high of 161.96 - a break sends the pair to levels last seen in 1986. Japan Finance Minister Katayama renewed intervention warnings and reportedly held an undisclosed call with US Treasury Secretary Bessent. Oil fell over 30 percent across May-June as Hormuz traffic genuinely normalized toward pre-conflict levels per the US Energy Secretary, though a fresh incident this week saw a projectile strike the container vessel Ever Lovely, and Iran's parliament accused the US of violating the memorandum by stoking Hormuz tensions and backing an Israeli agent. Separately, Israel and Lebanon reportedly signed a preliminary deal with a US-facilitated trilateral military coordination framework, a genuinely positive parallel development. Australian May CPI showed a mixed signal - headline cooled to 4.0 percent largely on a fuel excise cut and lower oil prices, but trimmed-mean core rose to 3.6 percent, the highest since September 2024. Fresh CFTC data (period ending June 23) confirmed three major positioning shifts: AUD flipped to outright net short for the first time in this series (-13,012, -8,887 WoW), completing the multi-week unwind thesis; GBP shorts accelerated violently (-105,719, -34,134 WoW), the steepest single-week move recorded for any currency in this series; CAD remained at the most extreme single reading in the entire series (-146,792). USD/CAD long remains the clear Priority 1 setup with three consecutive weeks of confirmation and a series-high D1 ADX reading of 50.62. AUD/USD short thesis has fully completed and awaits explicit confirmation on new levels or closure. A new GBP/USD short signal is flagged on the extreme COT acceleration but awaits a named Thesis Invalidation before promotion to a full setup. USD/JPY has no active directional thesis currently - the original short setup expired weeks ago and the pair's approach to the historic 1986 trigger level awaits fresh direction.
highlights: Two-week gap, dollar rallied 2pct since FOMC
USD/JPY 161.92, one tick from 1986 trigger level
AUD COT flipped net short, unwind thesis completed
GBP COT shorts steepest single-week move in series
CAD COT -146,792, most extreme reading in series
NFP Thursday first major test of new Fed regime

title: Friday Weekly Synthesis - June 19, 2026. Warsh Eliminates Forward Guidance. Dollar to One-Year High. Iran MOU Signing Ceremony Today.
thesis: FOMC held 12-0 but markets read it hawkish - dollar hit one-year high as Warsh eliminated forward guidance entirely, submitted no Chair dot, cut the statement to roughly 150 words, and launched five task forces (communications, balance sheet, data sources, AI/productivity, inflation framework) to rebuild Fed operations from first principles. SCP showed a fractured 50/50 committee split on rate direction by year-end - Warsh described colleagues' dots as submitted with pencils and big erasers. Reaffirmed 2% target as non-negotiable. S&P 500 fell 1.2pct, yields rose. This is a structural change expected to produce materially higher volatility on every future data release. SNB held at 0.00pct, nudged inflation forecast to 0.6pct for 2026 on oil, raised FX intervention willingness against CHF strength. BoE held at 3.75pct - Bailey's pooled interview revealed genuine three-part caution despite being encouraged by Iran de-escalation: needs to see peace settle, needs energy-infrastructure damage assessment, needs to confirm inflation persistence given the UK's softened backdrop. Direct review of official RBA and BoJ statements produced two corrections to earlier mid-week characterizations: RBA's hold was genuinely balanced and explicitly retains hawkish optionality, not a dovish capitulation; BoJ's forward guidance was genuinely hawkish (explicit commitment to continue raising rates), with both dissents being hawkish rather than dovish. Iran has published a fully signed MOU, in effect immediately per Iran, with formal ceremony today at Burgenstock, Switzerland - confirmed terms include permanent ceasefire including Lebanon, Hormuz reopening with 30-day demining window, $300bn+ reconstruction fund, full sanctions removal schedule deferred to a 60-day final agreement requiring UN Security Council ratification. New domestic US political friction emerged this week as Trump allies publicly criticize the deal's sanctions relief scope, asset access terms, and fund size. ADX exploded across the board post-FOMC - EUR/USD H1 VERY STRONG 50.76, USD/CAD H1 VERY STRONG 59.90 with H4 also VERY STRONG 39.33 and D1 STRONG 40.21, confirming the most extreme multi-timeframe technical reading of the brief series. USD/CAD long elevated to clear Priority 1. AUD/USD short intact, fundamentally clarified as domestic-data-driven rather than RBA-dovishness-driven, though H1 lagging the week's broad dollar move. USD/JPY short's named Thesis Invalidation (BoJ no-hike) has passed without triggering since BoJ did hike - setup requires explicit new Thesis Invalidation or closure before carrying further, per standing rule against inventing replacement conditions.
highlights: Warsh eliminates forward guidance, no Chair dot
Dollar to one-year high despite FOMC hold
BoJ/RBA corrections from primary source review
Iran MOU formal signing today at Burgenstock
USD/CAD H1 VERY STRONG 59.9 - extreme reading
USD/JPY Thesis Invalidation expired - needs new TI

title: Midweek Brief - June 17, 2026. BoJ Hikes to 1.0% No Squeeze. RBA Holds Disappoints. FOMC Today. Iran MOU Signed, Ceremony Friday.
thesis: BoJ raised rates to 1.0% Tuesday as expected (7-1 vote, Ueda absent, highest since 1995) but JPY did not strengthen materially since no 50bp surprise was delivered. Wholesale inflation hit 6.3% in May confirming pass-through risk. RBA held Tuesday but AUD fell as statement lacked sufficient hawkish content given Iran de-escalation reducing inflation premium - swaps now price only 16bp further tightening through year-end. Q1 GDP 0.3% q/q, unemployment 4.5% (4.5-year high). Trump, Vance, and Iran's Ghalibaf electronically signed the US-Iran peace MOU - formal ceremony confirmed for Friday June 19 at Burgenstock, Switzerland. Trump stated Hormuz will be fully open by Friday. Structural analysis flags Iranian draft terms (blockade lift, sanctions suspension, Lebanon ceasefire, Hormuz reopening, $300bn reconstruction fund, $24bn frozen funds released) as more favorable to Iran than the US, with minimal substantive nuclear resolution and enriched uranium likely remaining in-country - working base case is ceasefire holds through summer/midterms with renewed escalation risk after. Oil collapsed to $75-80 on the de-escalation, removing the energy-driven inflation premium that anchored recent G10 hawkishness. FOMC decision today 18:00 GMT - Warsh's inaugural press conference, hold expected, oil collapse gives latitude for less hawkish tone. AUD/USD setup downgraded this session - ADX confirmation dropped from full 4-timeframe STRONG to D1/W1 only as Iran-deal risk-on bounce cost H1/H4 momentum; fundamental case intact, technical conviction weaker. USD/JPY short mechanism shifted from JPY-specific BoJ squeeze to broad USD weakness from Iran deal - original Thesis Invalidation event (BoJ no-hike) has now passed without triggering since BoJ did hike, requiring reframing next session. USD/CAD long facing first genuine two-sided test as oil collapse (CAD negative) offsets broad USD weakness (USD/CAD negative) - FOMC tonight is the resolver.
highlights: BoJ hiked to 1.0%, no JPY squeeze on no-surprise
RBA held, AUD fell on insufficient hawkishness
Iran MOU electronically signed, ceremony Friday

title: Sunday Weekly Debrief - June 14, 2026. Iran Deal Text Confirmed. JPY + CAD COT New Cycle Extremes. BoJ + FOMC Resolution Week.
thesis: Pakistan PM confirmed final agreed text of Iran-US peace deal reached Saturday. Markets pricing Hormuz reopening - risk-on impulse expected Monday open. Structural analysis: Iran non-negotiables (enrichment right, simultaneous sanctions removal, frozen assets, no missile talks, high-enriched uranium stays in Iran) remain incompatible with US demands - deal text is a framework not a durable resolution without Khamenei explicit endorsement. Fresh CFTC data (period ending June 9) shows three COT extremes all deepening simultaneously: JPY net short -145,818 (new cycle record, -16,251 WoW) heading into Tuesday confirmed BoJ hike to 1.0%; CAD net short -119,999 (new cycle record, -25,888 WoW, two consecutive massive builds); EUR long collapsed from +48,866 to +13,932 (-34,934 WoW, largest single-week EUR long liquidation of 2026); AUD long unwind accelerated to +18,160 from +41,812 (-23,652 WoW), approaching net neutral. USD/JPY H4 ADX moved to STRONG (26.61) - first H4 momentum signal. AUD/USD H4/D1/W1 all STRONG. USD/CAD D1 STRONG (33.24) strengthened. All three setups carry with zero Thesis Invalidation events triggered: USD/CAD long (Priority 2, D1 STRONG, CAD -119,999 extreme, FOMC Wednesday, TI BoC hawkish hold removing easing guidance); AUD/USD short (Priority 1, 3-TF momentum, COT approaching net neutral, Iran bounce = entry, TI RBA patience language restoration); USD/JPY short (Priority 3, H4 STRONG developing, COT -145,818 record into live BoJ, TI no hike plus removes inflation vigilance language). Week 25 is the most event-dense of 2026: BoJ Tuesday early GMT, RBA Tuesday, FOMC Wednesday 18:00 GMT, SNB Thursday, BoE Thursday, NZD GDP Wednesday, UK CPI Wednesday.
highlights: Pakistan PM confirms Iran-US deal text
JPY COT -145,818 new cycle record into BoJ
BoJ Tue + FOMC Wed resolve all 3 setups

title: Friday Weekly Synthesis - June 12, 2026. Week 24 Close. All Three Setups Intact. BoJ + FOMC June 16-17 Resolution Week.
thesis: Week 24 confirmed the inflation regime, validated all three setups, and delivered zero Thesis Invalidation events. CPI Wednesday 3.8% y/y with marginal core deceleration — CROFX verdict: pullback not regime break, dollar has space to 100.3-100.5. ECB hiked 25bp to 2.40% Thursday — Lagarde: broadening inflation and growth absent or under threat. ECB sources: projections embed two more hikes, September still possible. IMF raised EZ 2026 inflation to 2.8%. EUR spiked then faded on growth concerns. PPI Thursday printed above 6.5% annual — inflation pipeline confirmed. BoC held Wednesday dovish — USD/CAD Thesis Invalidation not triggered. RBA held Tuesday with June 4 language intact — AUD/USD Thesis Invalidation not triggered. Trump cancelled Iran strikes Thursday evening, stated deal signing maybe over weekend — EUR/USD +0.07%, oil brief pullback, effect reversed by Friday. Iran-Israel escalation cycle this week: Israel struck south Beirut, Iran launched ~10 ballistic missiles toward Israel, Israel struck Mahshahr petrochemical complex, Iran halted with explicit threat. Lebanon remains blocking condition. Hormuz structurally closed, July 9 CROFX urgency deadline. AUD/USD maintained H1/H4/D1/W1 all STRONG for five consecutive sessions — five-week streak of four-timeframe confirmation. USD/JPY H1 STRONG (28.61) developing ahead of BoJ. USD/CAD D1 STRONG (31.69) intact. Three setups carry into Week 25 BoJ + FOMC resolution: USD/CAD long (Priority 2, H4 close above 1.3960, TI BoC hawkish hold), AUD/USD short (Priority 1, H4 close below 0.7045, TI RBA patience language restoration), USD/JPY short (Priority 3, H4 rejection below 159.50, TI BoJ no hike plus removes inflation vigilance language).
highlights: ECB hiked to 2.40%, two more hikes embedded
All 3 setups intact, zero TI events

title: Midweek Brief - June 10, 2026. CPI Day. BoC Decision. Iran-Israel Largest Escalation Since April.
thesis: US CPI day with CROFX early estimates pointing to headline 4.2% (highest since June 2023) and core 3.0% — if confirmed, regime acceleration not a reaction trade. Markets currently pricing 30bp of Fed hikes through December 2026. BoC decision follows at 13:45 GMT, expected hold on labour market concern and USMCA uncertainty with new 10% US tariff on Canadian products. ECB hike expected Thursday June 11 — CROFX: EUR has no strength vs USD, only brief EUR spike possible if hawkish July guidance delivered. Nikkei reported BoJ intends to lift rates to 1.0% at June 16-17 meeting — USD/JPY reclaimed 160 wiping out five weeks of intervention effects. MoF waiting for BoJ rather than pre-emptively intervening. CROFX analysis: BoJ hike alone insufficient for JPY recovery, needs explicit forward hawkish guidance. Iran-Israel largest escalation since April ceasefire: Lebanon collapsed the framework — Israel struck south Beirut Hezbollah targets, Iran launched ~10 ballistic missiles toward northern Israel, Israel struck Iranian targets including Mahshahr petrochemical complex, Iran halted with explicit threat declaring US bases legitimate targets. Trump publicly broke with Netanyahu — first US-Israeli split of this cycle. Hormuz reopening narrative functionally dead. July 9 flagged as urgency deadline by CROFX. All three Sunday setups carry intact — no Thesis Invalidation events triggered. AUD/USD short elevated to Priority 1 on full 4-TF ADX confirmation (H1/H4/D1/W1 all STRONG) — only pair with four-timeframe momentum today. USD/CAD D1 STRONG intact, entry on H4 close above 1.3960 post-BoC. USD/JPY watching for bearish H4 rejection 159.50-160.00 ahead of June 16-17.
highlights: BoC hold expected, statement language binary
ECB hike Thu, EUR no USD strength

title: Sunday Weekly Debrief - June 7, 2026. NFP Blowout Reasserts USD Regime. Three High-Conviction Setups.
thesis: NFP May printed +172K vs +85K consensus - the regime-confirmation print that closed the Week 22 softening window entirely. December 2026 Fed hike now 63% priced vs 48% pre-print. Two G10 central banks turned formally hawkish via official documents inside five days - BoJ June 3 Comprehensive Policy Review removed large-scale easing framework and added upward inflation deviation vigilance language ahead of live June 16-17 meeting; RBA June 4 Senate statement removed patience language and added second-round pass-through references. Iran de-escalation fully reversed: Hormuz effectively closed per Japan MoF, US forces downed two Iranian drones threatening maritime traffic, drone struck Mina al Fahal oil terminal Oman, Iran fired ballistic missiles at Bahrain and Kuwait. ECB delivered June 4 insurance hike but EUR/USD all timeframes WEAK on ADX - institutional buying did not follow the hike. DXY closed 100.07. XAU/USD -3.10% to 4,365, XAG/USD -6.58% to 69.11. Three high-conviction setups: USD/CAD long (H1/H4/D1 all STRONG - only multi-TF confirmation in gauge, CAD COT -94,111 largest single-week short build this cycle, BoC June 10 binary trigger, TI: BoC hawkish hold removing easing guidance), AUD/USD short (13-year COT long extreme +41,812 with -18,343 WoW unwind beginning, H1 V.STRONG 41.46 H4 STRONG 29.77, TI: RBA June meeting restoring patience language), USD/JPY short (JPY COT extreme -129,567 deepening -14,900 WoW ahead of live BoJ June 16-17, no trend to fight on higher TFs, entry on bearish H4 rejection 159.50-160.00, TI: BoJ no hike and removes upward inflation deviation vigilance language). Triple-catalyst week: US CPI Wed June 10, BoC Wed June 10, ECB Thu June 11.
highlights: RBA Jun 4 Senate formal hawkish shift
CAD COT -94,111 largest build this cycle

title: Weekly Synthesis - June 5, 2026. Institutional Hawkish Shift Week. RBA + BoJ Both Formal Hawkish. Setup 4 TI Triggered.
thesis: Week 23 was the institutional hawkish-shift week with TWO G10 central banks moving formal positions hawkish via official documents inside five days. RBA Bullock Senate statement June 4 added second-round inflation pass-through language and underlying inflation above target until mid-2027, removed space-to-see-how-conflict-plays-out patience language, shifted forward guidance from a-bit-restrictive to well-placed-to-respond-Board-will-do-what-it-considers-necessary - the explicit hike-signaling guidance that triggered Setup 4 AUD/USD short runner Thesis Invalidation. Runner closed at breakeven per framework, prior 0.7080 partial remains booked - win on discipline. BoJ June 3 Comprehensive Policy Review added secondary spillover effects language and upward deviation in underlying inflation vigilance, removed large-scale monetary easing framework language - institutional hawkish pre-positioning ahead of June 16-17 meeting that dramatically reinforces Setup 1 USD/JPY short structural case. Iran de-escalation reversed mid-week: Iran withdrew from US message exchanges, attacked US targets in Kuwait/Bahrain, US forces struck Iranian Qeshm island, largest exchange of fire since April truce, Hormuz effectively closed per Japan MoF. Friday brought Hezbollah rejecting Israel-Lebanon ceasefire one day after agreement plus new drone strike on Mina al Fahal oil terminal in Oman - first Gulf petroleum infrastructure hit of escalation cycle. Yet oil settled at $78 with no panic bid - market reads as managed escalation around ongoing negotiations not start of wider war. NFP printed strong across spectrum confirming labor market recovery, headline beat, wages/unemployment USD positive - combined with inflation above 3% and GDP growth holding, all USD positive read. Pre-NFP dovish positioning from softer Thu claims/Challenger plus Daly either-direction commentary collapsed; USD bid reasserted. USD/JPY tested 160 with several sharp short drops suggesting suspected Japanese intervention; 160.50 stop survived. Setup 2 NZD/USD long stopped at 0.5870 mid-week as momentum-collapse risk flagged in Sunday post-build commentary and explicitly framed as judgment call in Wednesday brief materialized. Setup 3 XAU/USD long stayed gated and never triggered - gold broke $4,500 floor to $4,486 - gated discipline produced no loss. ECB delivered insurance hike Thursday but EUR all timeframes WEAK on ADX. USD/CAD recovered to 1.391 vindicating doubled CAD short COT positioning. CAD tinderbox tilted USD-favorable on deal stall. Next week light calendar before BoJ + FOMC June 16-17 double-header institutional resolution. Setup 1 carries as cleanest catalyst-positioning trade on book.
highlights: RBA Senate Jun 4 hawkish - Setup 4 TI triggered
BoJ Jun 3 Comprehensive Policy Review hawkish
Mina al Fahal Oman terminal drone strike
BoJ + FOMC Jun 16-17 the next resolver

title: Midweek Brief - June 3, 2026. The Regime Tip Paused, Book Under Pressure.
thesis: The Friday May 29 regime tip has paused, not deepened, putting the active book under real tactical pressure though no stops have hit. Trump has not signed the Iran MoU and is now demanding written nuclear concessions in a preliminary deal, extending the formalization timeline rather than closing it. US ISM Manufacturing rose to 54.5 from 52.3 (strongest in months) reinforcing the USD bid, German May preliminary CPI surprised soft at -0.2% m/m (vs +0.1% expected), Eurozone flash CPI held at 3.0% headline with core 2.2% setting up the ECB hike Thursday. Australian May CPI accelerated to 4.4% from 4.3% intensifying RBA hike bets - the AUD runner's TI risk is now elevated. NZD H1 ADX collapsed from V.STRONG 61.59 Sunday to NEUTRAL 23.32 Wednesday - the momentum signal that drove Setup 2 conviction has died, price within 50 pips of the 0.5870 stop, no TI formally triggered. USD/JPY short offside at 159.5-159.8 but BoJ June 16-17 catalyst intact and COT cycle-extreme short -114,667 still loaded. Gold broke through $4,500 floor to $4,486 (-0.75%), Setup 3 gated never triggered (no loss), conditions receded. USD/CAD recovered to 1.384 vindicating the doubled-CAD-short COT positioning; no fresh position taken on the tinderbox. ECB Thursday and NFP Friday are the catalysts that resolve the partial reversal. The NZD/USD long is the judgment-call setup: framework letter says hold to stop, framework spirit (momentum died) leans toward closing on tactical weakness. Fed long-pause under Warsh remains the base case.
highlights: Trump not signed, nuclear concessions added
AU CPI 4.4% accelerated, RBA hike risk
ECB Thu and NFP Fri the resolvers

title: Sunday Brief - May 31, 2026. COT Confirms the Rotation, MoU Awaits Trump Signature.
thesis: Fresh CFTC release week ending May 26 printed three positioning extremes plus one significant outlier confirming the rotated book. JPY net short hit a new cycle extreme -114,667 (was -93,905, shorts +27,152) - the squeeze fuel is loaded heavier than at any prior point this cycle into BoJ June 16-17. CAD short nearly doubled to -68,882 (was -31,231, net change -37,651) - specs went max bearish into the Iran-deal narrative, creating a tinderbox that resolves on the Trump MoU decision and Wednesday BoC. AUD record long finally unwinding to +60,155 from +85,644 (longs -22,454) - the crowded-position-unwind thesis playing out, validates Setup 4 runner no-add rule. NZD short covered to -34,179 from -40,613 - specs rotating bullish into hawkish RBNZ, confirms Setup 2 long. Gold longs normalized to +154,260 - less crowded, healthier setup for gated Setup 3. Iran 60-day MoU was agreed Thursday covering Hormuz reopening, blockade lift, no-nuclear commitment, but Trump asked few days to consider and weekend brought friction: Trump demands unrestricted Hormuz access (rejecting Iran-Oman joint management), blockade still active, Bessent signals more action possible. Sunday ADX: NZD H1 V.STRONG 61.59 highest on board, USD/JPY H1 STRONG 33.49 building, XAU H1 V.STRONG 44.35 momentum returning. Book carries three actives (USD/JPY short cycle extreme, NZD/USD long firing, XAU/USD long gated above $4,640) plus AUD runner. EUR stand aside until ECB Thursday. USD/CAD no fresh position - tinderbox resolves on Trump statement plus BoC Wed.
highlights: ECB Thu and NFP Fri the resolvers

title: Friday Weekly Synthesis - May 29, 2026. The Week the Regime Tipped on Soft PCE and Iran Memorandum.
thesis: The regime tipped on two parallel forces landing simultaneously Friday. US Core PCE printed +0.2% mm vs +0.3% expected (3.3% YoY firm) - mild monthly miss, not material, taking the edge off the most hawkish Fed scenarios without breaking the regime. Treasury Secretary Bessent publicly endorsed a Hormuz transit-toll arrangement and Iranian state media published initial Memorandum of Understanding details covering US military withdrawal from Iran's vicinity, Navy lifting the Hormuz blockade, and Iranian commitments on commercial transit numbers. Oil collapsed from ~$103 Wed to $89.32 Fri - the cleanest market vote on deal credibility. DXY tested 99 lower, dollar broadly weakened. US Q1 GDP second estimate revised lower confirming growth-narrowing thesis. Week scorecard: USD/CAD long WIN on trailed stop 1.3740 locking ~30 pips before the selloff, USD/JPY standby correct no-entry, NZD short stood aside vindicated, EUR and AUD shorts scratched without stops hit or TIs fully triggered. Next week three active setups: USD/JPY short activating from standby on triple catalyst (BoJ ~75% June hike + de-escalation + soft PCE), XAU/USD long gated re-entry above $4,640 (conditions converging), NZD/USD long flip on hawkish RBNZ + risk-on (H1 V.STRONG 47.99 highest on board). AUD runner held passively no add. EUR stood aside until cleaner signal. USD/CAD closed no re-entry. ECB Thu Jun 4 and NFP Fri Jun 5 are the regime-durability tests; BoJ + FOMC Jun 16-17 the next institutional inflection. Warsh reportedly avoiding public until post-FOMC press conference. USMCA revision Jul 1 rotates trade story back to fore.

title: Midweek Brief - May 27, 2026. RBNZ Hawkish, BoJ Squeeze Building, USD/CAD Long the Clean Winner.
thesis: Holiday-shortened week building to Friday US Core PCE, with risks skewed up given the hot CPI/PPI precedent and base effects. RBNZ delivered a hawkish hold with roughly three hikes now priced by year-end and July at 80% - NZD re-rated higher, so the low-conviction COT NZD short is stood aside. BoJ Ueda turned hawkish with ~75% odds of a June 16-17 hike, lifting the yen and pushing USD/JPY H1 ADX to STRONG 30.91 - the squeeze case is forming early ahead of the catalyst, against the cycle-extreme COT short -93,905. AU CPI softened to 3.7% (below 3.8% expected), mildly dovish, reinforcing the AUD short. Eurozone composite PMI fell deeper into contraction at 47.5, Q1 GDP missed, German confidence at multi-year low. Iran re-escalated with new US strikes even as negotiations continue; oil rallied toward $105 then capped. USD/CAD long remains the featured clean winner in profit at ~1.380, H4 ADX V.STRONG 44.49 for a fourth reading, resilient even to the oil rally because the broad dollar bid and doubled CAD short dominate. EUR/USD and AUD/USD shorts rested on momentum but fundamentals held; both sell-rally/sell-bounce into PCE. Stop trailed up on USD/CAD toward 1.3740.
highlights: US Core PCE Fri the anchor

title: Sunday Brief - May 24, 2026. COT Extremes Confirm USD Complex, Iran Draft the Swing Factor.
thesis: CFTC week ending May 19 printed multiple positioning extremes: JPY net short hit a fresh cycle extreme -93,905 (shorts +25,251), CAD net short nearly doubled to -31,231 confirming the USD/CAD long that has the board's strongest momentum (H4 V.STRONG 44.9), GBP short rebuilt to -64,307, AUD held a record long +85,644, USD Index flipped net short -479 even as the dollar rallied, gold longs trimmed to +159,833. Positioning and price structure converge this week so COT signals and Top Setups reinforce. Weekend Iran development is the swing factor: US-Iran reacted positively to an agreement draft via Pakistani mediation and oil dropped to ~$92, against a CENTCOM critical-stage statement and Iranian highest-readiness declaration - a firm deal plus sustained oil selling would unwind the safe-haven dollar bid and threaten the EUR and AUD shorts while helping USD/CAD. Equities sold off ~4% on the week into ~7,050-7,100 as the short positioning expressed. Memorial Day Monday US closed; US Core PCE Friday May 29 the anchor with Eurozone flash CPI the same day. Four Top Setups: USD/CAD long featured (cons 1.3950, agg 1.4135+), EUR/USD short (target 1.1450/1.1400, SL 1.1710), AUD/USD short (cons 0.6950, agg 0.6800), USD/JPY squeeze-standby into BoJ June 17 (cons 155.50, agg 152.50). Five COT signals provided with full entry/zone/target/stop/thesis-invalidation.
highlights: US Core PCE Fri the anchor

title: Friday Weekly Synthesis - May 22, 2026. Inflation Regime Institutionally Anchored, USD/JPY to Standby.
thesis: The week confirmed and institutionally anchored the inflation regime: FOMC minutes showed more policymakers open to a rate hike, December hike priced 40-50%, DXY logged an eighth consecutive higher-high session. AU unemployment spiked to 4.5% (4.5-year high), US flash PMIs strong (manufacturing 55.3) while Eurozone PMIs stayed soft, Japan core CPI undershot. Beneath the strong headlines the quality of US growth is narrowing - consumption concentrated in energy and gasoline while discretionary sectors weaken, a stagflation signature with energy masking a tiring consumer. Week scorecard: EUR/USD short WIN in profit, AUD/USD short WIN partial booked at 0.7080 with free runner, NZD/USD and GBP/USD shorts scratched into consolidation, USD/JPY short the featured miss as the pair ground toward 160 against ineffective intervention. Every named-event Thesis Invalidation behaved correctly. Next week May 25-29: Memorial Day Monday US closed, US Core PCE Friday the anchor, Eurozone flash CPI, US GDP revision. Three active setups carried: EUR/USD short (SL 1.1710), USD/CAD long (rotate-in, H4 V.STRONG 40.69), AUD/USD short (re-load on bounce). USD/JPY moved to standby into BoJ June 17.
highlights: USDJPY to standby into BoJ Jun 17
US Core PCE Fri the anchor

title: Midweek Brief - May 20, 2026. UK CPI Sticky, FOMC Minutes Tonight, Risk-Off Arriving.
thesis: UK core CPI printed 4.2% sticky but BoE hike pricing did NOT revive - markets still price cuts. UK unemployment rose to ~4.9-5.0%, wages slowing, 30Y gilts above 5%, Starmer political crisis deepening. FOMC Minutes pending tonight 18:00 GMT - first Warsh-era read, expected 8-4 split. Despite hawkish headline, market only prices 13-17bp tightening by Dec, first full 25bp by April 2027 - USD strength is safe-haven plus yields, not aggressive Fed. Risk-off arriving: SPX -0.7% third down day, 2.2% below ATH, VIX short-squeeze setup expressing. 10Y at 4.60%, 30Y 5.15-5.20%. Gold capitulation accelerating to H4 ADX V.STRONG 46.05, spot $4,470. USD/JPY above 159, MoF intervention pre-announced and imminent. Four of five Sunday setups in profit: EUR/USD near 1.15, NZD/USD to 0.5700, AUD/USD partial booked at 0.7080 runner to 0.6950, GBP/USD held alive. USD/JPY Option A active, Option B missed.

title: Sunday Weekly Debrief - May 17, 2026. CPI/PPI/Retail Sales Confirmed Regime, Warsh Assumed.
thesis: Three consecutive prints confirmed inflation as the regime not a question: CPI 0.4% core mm Tuesday, PPI 6.0% YoY four-year high Wednesday with Core PPI +1.0% mm, Retail Sales Control Group beat Thursday. 10Y at 4.51% highest since June 2025, 30Y at 5.056% fresh 10-month high, Japan 10Y at 1997 levels. Warsh assumed Fed Chair Friday with hawkish repricing already done. CFTC week ending May 12: JPY shorts REBUILT to -75,102 (was -61,738), AUD record long +84,990, GBP covered hard to -43,059 but cable kept falling, NZD covered to -39,150, USD Idx building +3,187, EUR longs trap +40,200. Equity Index COT: S-P 500 specs -143.8K, VIX shorts DOUBLED -49.0K. Setups 1, 3, 4 in profit. Setup 3 XAU/USD long stopped clean at $4,640 by named-event Thesis Invalidation. Setup 5 GBP/USD short added. Setup 2 USD/JPY shown with both directional options. Three Week Ahead scenarios tied to UK CPI Wed, FOMC Minutes Wed, AU Employment Thu, and Iran tail risk.

title: Weekly Synthesis - May 15, 2026. CPI Hot, PPI 6.0% 4-Year High, Warsh Assumes.
thesis: Three consecutive hot prints confirmed the inflation regime: CPI 3.8% hd / 0.4% core mm Tuesday (right at Sunday-flagged threshold), PPI 6.0% YoY four-year high Wednesday with Core PPI +1.0% mm, Retail Sales Control Group beat Thursday. 10Y broke to 4.51% (highest since June 2025), 30Y to 5.056% fresh 10-month high, Japan 10Y at 1997 levels. Warsh assumed Fed Chair Friday inheriting hawkish repricing. Setup 3 XAU/USD long stopped at $4,640 (gold $4,572). Setups 1, 3 in profit; Setup 2 USDJPY entry zone live at MoF intervention. New Setup 4 AUDUSD short on crowded +78K COT long unwind. Trump-Xi closed with no Iran resolution.

title: Midweek Brief - May 13, 2026. CPI Hot, Regime Flipped.
thesis: US CPI printed hot - Headline 3.8% vs 3.7%, Core m/m 0.4% vs 0.3% - right at the Sunday-flagged threshold. Fed cuts removed, 17bp of HIKES priced through April 2027. DXY recovered to 98.275 from 97.84. Iran-US ceasefire 'on life support' per Trump, oil $100-102. Sunday Setup 1 EUR/USD long thesis invalidated. Setup 2 USD/JPY short thesis strengthened (BoJ June hike 70%). Setup 3 gold held with only -$9. New Setup 4 NZD/USD short added - H4 ADX collapsed back to WEAK confirming last week's NZD strength was noise. PPI today and Retail Sales Thursday are referendum on CPI thesis.

title: Sunday Weekly Debrief - May 10, 2026
thesis: DXY broke 98 to 97.84 on NFP composition weakness. JPY shorts collapsed -102K to -61.7K (+40K cover) - post-intervention squeeze halfway through. CAD covered hard -38.5K to -14.7K. Gold extended to $4,715 with W1 ADX V.STRONG 40.80. CPI Tuesday May 12 is the binary event for everything bullish. Warsh confirmation Mon, Powell term ends Fri.

title: Weekly Synthesis. NFP Today. US-Iran Escalation. EUR H4 Strong.
thesis: NFP consensus +62K vs 178K prior - first potential evidence energy shock hitting employment. US-Iran ceasefire Thursday fully reversed by military exchanges Friday. EUR/USD H1+H4 both Strong for first time. USD/JPY H4 ADX 47.1 V.Strong increasing. Australia trade deficit -A$1,841M shock complicates AUD/NZD Signal #4. Gold W1 40.8 V.Strong fifth consecutive week.

title: Midweek Brief. RBA 4.35% Third Hike. ECB June Inevitable. ADP Today.
thesis: RBA hiked to 4.35% (8-1), third hike of 2026, confirmed second-round effects. ECB Kazimir: June tightening all but inevitable. AUD H1 ADX 40.0 V.Strong. USD/JPY H4 ADX 43.61 V.Strong from intervention. ADP 12:15 GMT is the USD catalyst. NFP Friday defines the week.

title: Sunday Debrief. JPY -102,059 Despite Intervention. CFTC COT. CB Week Synthesis.
thesis: JPY net short deepened to -102,059 DESPITE Japan's $34.5B intervention - specs bought the dip. AUD +71,869 building. New Signal #4: AUD/NZD BUY with 118K COT divergence. FOMC correction: easing bias was retained, not removed. RBA May 5 hike and NFP May 8 are the week's catalysts. Warsh chair term begins May 15.

title: Weekly Synthesis. Japan Intervenes. FOMC Hawkish. ECB 3 Hikes. BoE Dovish.
thesis: Japan confirmed FX intervention - USD/JPY ADX H1 hit 63.88. FOMC 8-4 removed 2026 cuts, 160bps Fed-ECB gap. ECB hawkish pivot: 3 hikes priced. BoE dovish surprise: 75bps cuts priced 2026. EUR/CAD and EUR/NZD signals strengthened. RBA May 5 hike next catalyst.

title: Midweek Brief. BoJ Hawkish Hold 6-3. FOMC Today. ECB and BoE Tomorrow.
thesis: BoJ held 0.75% but three dissenters voted for 1.0%. Core CPI revised to 2.8%. June hike confirmed as base case. EUR below 1.1700. USD/CHF is momentum pair (ADX H1+H4 Strong). Triple decision day: AUD CPI, BoC, FOMC. ECB and BoE tomorrow resolve all COT signals.

title: Sunday Debrief. JPY -94,460 Extreme. CFTC COT Data. ADX Momentum Gauge.
thesis: JPY net short at 19-year extreme -94,460. AUD confirmed net long +64,817 from CFTC raw data. EUR COT +41,324. CAD -58,834. Five central bank decisions in 72 hours - BoJ Apr 28, BoC Apr 29, FOMC Apr 29, ECB + BoE Apr 30. EUR/CAD BUY is top signal. Gold weekly ADX 46.1 - Very Strong.

title: Weekly Synthesis. EUR Corrects 1.5%. Gold -2.5%. DXY Recovers. PMI Miss.
thesis: EUR/USD fell from 1.1849 high to 1.1665 on ZEW miss, PMI contraction, and USD safe-haven bid. Gold corrected to $4,688. Oil +10% on Hormuz uncertainty. Central bank cascade Apr 28-30 resolves all four COT signals - BoJ, BoC, ECB, BoE in 72 hours.

title: Midweek Brief. UK CPI 3.3% Beat. ZEW Miss. COT Signals Reassessed.
thesis: UK CPI 3.3% y/y beat BoE hike near-certain Apr 30. Germany ZEW -17.2 miss improves EUR/CAD entry. NZ CPI 3.1% y/y complicated EUR/NZD and NZD/JPY signals - paused not cancelled. Flash PMIs Thursday are the macro checkpoint.

title: Monday Brief. Hormuz Re-closed. Iran Refuses Talks. Ueda Dovish G20.
thesis: Hormuz re-closed Saturday, Iran fired on tankers, US seized Iranian vessel Monday. Risk-off gap open. Ueda G20 dovish remarks collapsed BoJ April hike odds to 15%. CAD CPI 12:30 GMT is COT Signal timing trigger. EUR/CAD and EUR/NZD COT signals improving on risk-off dip.

title: Sunday Debrief. EUR CFTC Flip +33,559. JPY Squeeze. Hormuz Whipsaw.
thesis: EUR flipped to net long +33,559 - largest repositioning of the year. JPY covering from -20,870 to +10,534. USD basket -18,555 net short. Hormuz opened Friday then closed Saturday. Top signals: EUR/CAD buy, EUR/NZD buy, CAD/JPY sell, NZD/JPY sell.

title: Weekly Synthesis. COT Regime Flip. AUD and GBP Net Short. USD Bounce.
thesis: AUD flipped to -10,693 net short while price hit 4-year high - classic distribution. GBP flipped to -3,689. JPY shorts deepened to -20,870. USD bounced Friday on oil rebound to $94. De-escalation trade fully priced. BoJ Apr 28 and ECB Apr 30 are the binary events.

title: Easter Catch-Up Sunday Debrief. Apr 9-14. Hormuz Blockade Active.
thesis: Hormuz naval blockade confirmed Apr 13. Full whipsaw week - ceasefire dissolved, new blockade imposed. RBNZ hawkish pivot suspended NZD bearish thesis. DXY at 6-week low 98.36. EUR/USD pushed to 1.1787.

title: Ceasefire 2.0 + RBNZ Pivot. Oil -16%. Gold $4,813. NZD Thesis Revised.
thesis: Trump 2-week conditional ceasefire triggers risk-on. Oil -16%. Gold +2.3%. RBNZ hawkish hold with 4.2% inflation forecast - NZD short thesis suspended. COT AUD/CAD and EUR/GBP both confirmed. FOMC minutes 2pm EST today.

title: Weekly Debrief. Trump Reversed Apr 2. Silver -6.75%. NFP +178k. DXY Below 100.
thesis: De-escalation lasted 24 hours. Trump vowed intensified strikes Apr 2. Silver crashed -6.75%. Strong NFP 178k but DXY broke below 100 - markets pricing stagflation not Fed hikes. Iran struck South Pars Saturday.

title: Iran De-Escalation Signal. S-P +2.91%. Gold $4,720.
thesis: Trump confirms US operations end in 2-3 weeks. Biggest equity day since May. Gold at 8-day high on USD weakness. Hormuz still closed - relief rally not regime change.

title: MRKT System Down. USDJPY 160.25. Gold +2.96%.
thesis: Iran conflict oil shock locks Fed into stagflationary hold. Q1 end rebalancing adds noise. AUD weakest major on CPI miss. USDJPY at intervention trigger level.

title: Iran Deadline Extension. Gold $4,507. Equities Correction.
thesis: Geopolitical oil shock triggering stagflationary regime shift. Fed hawkish hold, 52% rate hike odds, S-P 500 breaks 200-DMA. Gold dual-demand bid.
